But, if you're looking for a similar plant, there is a plant called Psorothamnus arborescens, which is also known as "creosote bush". Here's a paragraph about it:

Psorothamnus arborescens, also known as creosote bush, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The health benefits of Psorothamnus arborescens include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Psorothamnus arborescens include terpenoids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psorothamnus arborescens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
